{"key_event":"Belgian annexation and bureaucratic extractive expansion","reasoning":"After 1908, Belgian annexation regularized administration, courts, taxation, the Force Publique, transport links, and mining/plantation concessions, raising state reach from a brutal but uneven Leopoldian regime to a more bureaucratic colonial state. Congolese society remained extremely atomized: anti-rubber reform pressure came mainly from Europe, while forced labor, taxation, disease, demographic loss, and suppression of revolts further weakened local autonomy, with missions creating only very limited associational capacity.","society_change":-0.2,"society_power":1.2,"state_change":1.0,"state_power":6.0}
